items.title: vLLM-Ascend v0.22.1rc1(HCCL 权重传输后端打通 RL 流水线)
items.innovation: 基于 HCCL 的权重传输后端,RL 训推权重同步摆脱 CUDA/NCCL 依赖
items.analysis: Innovation: 对齐上游 vLLM v0.22.1,新增基于 HCCL 的权重传输后端,使 RL 训练-推理流水线的权重同步不再依赖 CUDA/NCCL;Mooncake connector 支持 DeepSeek V4 混合 KV cache 的 PD 分离,扩展 Ascend 950 W8A8/W4A8 动态量化,新增 Kimi 2.6、GLM-5.2 支持。Performance: split_qkv_tp_rmsnorm_rope 算子在 MiniMax-M2.5 W8A8 prefill 负载上约 5 倍 kernel 加速;已知问题为 MiniMax 2.7 双节点高负载可能挂起。对 RL-on-NPU 的意义: HCCL 原生权重同步是 RL-on-Ascend 的关键基础设施补齐——rollout 引擎与训练器之间的权重更新链路彻底去 NCCL 化,与 AReaL 2.0 独立出的权重更新服务恰好互补,直接服务本看板核心主线。
items.summary: v0.22.1rc1(6-30):新增 HCCL 权重传输后端打通 RL 训推权重同步、Mooncake connector 支持 V4 混合 KV cache PD 分离、Ascend 950 W8A8/W4A8 动态量化扩展、Kimi 2.6 / GLM-5.2 支持,以及 MiniMax-M2.5 prefill ~5x kernel 加速。

items.title: openPangu-2.0-Flash 开源(昇腾原生 MoE + 训练算子/后训练代码分批放出)
items.innovation: 昇腾原生模型首次连同训练算子与后训练代码体系开源
items.analysis: Innovation: 6 月 30 日起华为在 GitCode ascend-tribe 分批开源 openPangu 2.0 七大组件,首发 openPangu-2.0-Flash——稀疏 MoE(报道口径 92B 总参/6B 激活,另有 9.2B/0.6B 说法,以 GitCode 仓库为准)、512K 上下文,本次打包放出模型权重、基础推理代码与训推算子;开源范围还包括预训练代码与后训练(post-training)代码,505B/A18B 的 Pro 版权重定于 7 月开源。Performance: 华为自报昇腾单卡吞吐约为业界主流开源模型 2 倍(self-reported)。对 RL-on-NPU 的意义: 这是 RL-on-Ascend 工具链最直接的一手参考栈——昇腾上的后训练(RL/对齐)代码与训练算子首次成体系开源,openPangu 全系完全在昇腾 910B 上训练,可直接对照研究 NPU 上的 RL 实现细节。
items.summary: 华为正式开源 openPangu-2.0-Flash(稀疏 MoE、512K 上下文、昇腾原生),权重+推理代码+训推算子同步上线 GitCode;openPangu 2.0 七大组件(含预训练与后训练代码)分批开源,Pro 版(505B/A18B)7 月放出。参数规格各媒体口径不一,需以仓库为准。

items.title: Atlas 950 SuperPod 出海韩国(4Q26,单集群 8192 卡)
items.innovation: 昇腾集群首次成规模出海,从国产替代走向 NVIDIA 替代出口
items.analysis: What it is: TrendForce 援引韩媒报道,华为计划 2026 年四季度将昇腾 950 系列与 Atlas 950 SuperPod 引入韩国,单套部署最多 8192 颗 Ascend 950 加速器,宣称推理性能约为 NVIDIA H20 的 3 倍、成本约 1/4(转述数字,需谨慎);已与 Hansol PNS、SK Shieldus 签总代协议,推进技术培训、定价与本地化营销。Why it matters: 昇腾生态首次成规模出口,意味着 950 系列供给面与软件栈成熟度将接受海外客户检验。对 RL-on-NPU 的意义: 8192 卡 SuperPod 若如期落地,是大规模训推分离 RL(独立 rollout/train 资源池)在 Ascend 上的目标织物,也扩大了昇腾工具链的用户与反馈基数。
items.summary: 华为拟 4Q26 在韩国推出昇腾 950 芯片与 Atlas 950 SuperPod(单集群最多 8192 颗 950),宣称推理性能约 H20 的 3 倍、成本约 1/4(转述);已签 Hansol PNS、SK Shieldus 总代。昇腾从国产替代走向出口的标志性信号。

items.title: 寒武纪市值破万亿(思元 690 训练适配加速)
items.innovation: 昇腾之外最强国产 NPU 玩家的资本与大厂采购信号
items.analysis: What it is: 寒武纪 A 股市值首破 1 万亿元;背景是 2026 年以来字节、阿里、腾讯、百度等加大国产 AI 芯片采购并批量部署于大模型训练与 AIGC,其新旗舰思元 690 双 die 封装、FP16 算力超 700 TFLOPS、196GB HBM3(厂商此前公布数字),训练侧已支持 DeepSeek V3/V3.1、Qwen3 系列等 MoE 模型。Why it matters: 国产芯片训练生态整体升温,DeepSeek-V4 发布时寒武纪亦提供 Day-0 支持。对 RL-on-NPU 的意义: 看板国产 NPU 竞争格局的重要坐标——若思元 690 的训练侧 MoE 支持继续完善,RL-on-国产芯片将不再是昇腾单极,跨 NPU 后端抽象(HCCL 之外)会成为 RL 框架的新命题。
items.summary: 寒武纪 A 股市值首破 1 万亿元:字节、阿里、腾讯、百度加大国产 AI 芯片采购,思元 690(双 die、FP16 >700 TFLOPS、196GB HBM3)训练侧已支持 DeepSeek V3/V3.1、Qwen3 等 MoE 模型。国产 NPU 训练生态升温的资本面信号。

items.title: AscendCraft (auto Ascend kernel gen)
items.innovation: DSL-guided transcompilation auto-generates Ascend NPU kernels
items.analysis: Innovation: auto-generates Ascend NPU kernels via DSL-guided transcompilation, so missing operators don't each need a hand-written Ascend C kernel. Performance: targets the long-tail op-coverage gap that bottlenecks porting GPU stacks to Ascend (speedups paper-reported). Fit for RL-on-NPU: directly attacks the 'operator/kernel coverage gaps' challenge on this dashboard — fewer manual kernels means faster bring-up of RL/attention ops on 910B.
items.summary: Automatic Ascend NPU kernel generation through DSL-guided transcompilation — aimed at the long-tail operator gap that forces manual Ascend C kernels when porting CUDA stacks.

items.title: KVComp: sparse attention for vLLM-Ascend
items.innovation: KV-compression sparse attention with custom NPU kernels
items.analysis: Innovation: brings KV-compression sparse attention to vLLM-Ascend with custom NPU kernels (hamming_dist_top_k for top-k indices, reshape_and_cache_BNSD for cache layout). Status: community RFC/Issue #5507, on the 2026 roadmap. Fit for RL-on-NPU: sparse-attention rollout cuts decode memory/latency on memory-bound 910B — the practical NPU counterpart to DSA/MSA and a lever against the missing vLLM sleep-mode.
items.summary: RFC (Issue #5507) bringing KV-compression sparse attention to vLLM-Ascend via custom kernels (hamming_dist_top_k, reshape_and_cache_BNSD) — fills the long-context inference gap on the 2026 Ascend roadmap.

items.title: xMSDA (deformable attention on Ascend)
items.innovation: High-performance multi-scale deformable attention on Ascend (co-design)
items.summary: A co-design implementation of multi-scale deformable attention tuned for Ascend NPUs — an example of squeezing non-standard attention ops onto NPU hardware.

items.title: Ascend 950PR / 950DT
items.innovation: Next-gen Ascend with self-built HBM and native low-precision (FP8/MXFP4)
items.analysis: Innovation: first Ascend generation with Huawei's own HBM and native low-precision formats (FP8/MXFP8/HiF8/MXFP4) — 950PR (inference, ~Q1 2026) and 950DT (training/decode, ~Q4 2026; 144GB HBM @ ~4TB/s). Performance: ~1 PFLOPS FP8 / ~2 PFLOPS MXFP4 per analyst/vendor reporting — treat as approximate until silicon ships. Fit for RL-on-NPU: native FP8 directly unlocks FP8 RL training on Ascend (a near-empty research area), and the self-built HBM eases the memory bottleneck that currently forces rollout/train time-slicing on 910B.
items.summary: Announced at Huawei Connect 2025. 950PR (inference, ~Q1 2026) pairs HiBL 1.0 HBM (128 GB, ~1.6 TB/s); 950DT (training/decode, ~Q4 2026) uses HiZQ 2.0 (144 GB, ~4 TB/s, 2 TB/s interconnect). Adds FP8/MXFP8/HiF8/MXFP4 — ~1 PFLOPS FP8, ~2 PFLOPS MXFP4. Directly enables FP8 RL on Ascend. Specs from vendor/analyst reporting — treat as approximate.

items.title: Atlas 950 SuperPoD (8,192× 950DT)
items.innovation: 8,192-NPU supernode — successor to CloudMatrix 384
items.analysis: Innovation: scales the supernode from 384 (CM384/910C) to up to 8,192 Ascend 950DT chips over the UnifiedBus interconnect (~16 PB/s aggregate). Performance: ~8 EFLOPS FP8 / ~16 EFLOPS FP4 system-level (vendor). Fit for RL-on-NPU: the same 'scale to compensate for per-chip efficiency' play as CM384, but with native FP8 and self-built HBM — the target fabric for large disaggregated RL (separate rollout/train pools) at frontier scale. Numbers are vendor roadmap; treat as approximate until it ships (~Q4 2026).
items.summary: Announced at Huawei Connect 2025. Up to 8,192 Ascend 950DT NPUs (≈20x the Atlas 900 A3 / CM384 node count) on the UnifiedBus interconnect (~16 PB/s), delivering ~8 EFLOPS FP8 / ~16 EFLOPS FP4. Part of the 950(2026)→960(2027)→970(2028) roadmap that roughly doubles compute each generation.

items.title: vLLM-Ascend v0.18.0 (latest stable)
items.innovation: Latest Ascend rollout backend — broader model coverage + perf
items.summary: vLLM-Ascend reached v0.18.0 (May 2026), the production-recommended stable release — broader model coverage and performance after large-scale Expert Parallelism (v0.9.1, Sep 2025). It migrates attention/sampling ops to CANN, adapts Ascend's memory pool, and optimizes HCCS comms. The rollout engine RL stacks (verl / MindSpeed-RL) target on NPU; the sleep/offload gap vs CUDA vLLM remains the key constraint.

items.title: vLLM-Ascend model support: V4 / GLM-5 / Qwen3.5 / MiniMax (2026)
items.innovation: Latest frontier models running on 910B via vLLM-Ascend
items.summary: Per the 2026 support matrix: DeepSeek-V4 is supported on 910B with custom operators, KV-pool adaptation, and separated MTP-layer KV-cache sharding for speculative decoding; GLM-5 has perf optimizations + W8A8C8 quant + optimized KV cache; Qwen3.5 is supported (incl. LoRA on dense models); W8A8 quant spans the Qwen3 / DeepSeek / MiniMax families (MiniMax-M2.x via W8A8/QuaRot). MiniMax-M3 is not yet listed by name. Inference support generally lands first; training-side (verl / MindSpeed-RL) trails.

items.title: Ascend 910B
items.innovation: Mainstream domestic training NPU; ~A100-class in practice
items.summary: ~256–280 TFLOPS BF16, 64 GB HBM2e at ~1.2 TB/s, SMIC 7nm; ships in Atlas 800T A2 servers (8 NPUs/node). The most widely-available Ascend part for training through 2024–2025.
items.analysis: What it is: the mainstream domestic training NPU (~256–280 TFLOPS BF16, 64GB HBM2e, 8/node in Atlas 800T A2), roughly A100-class in practice. Why it matters: it's the most available Ascend part and the hardware nearly all current RL-on-Ascend stacks (verl, MindSpeed-RL, vLLM-Ascend) validate against. Fit for RL-on-NPU: 64GB/NPU plus the missing sleep-mode make memory the binding constraint for GRPO — start with 0.5B–7B models and budget rollout/train memory carefully.

items.title: Ascend 910C
items.innovation: Dual-die flagship positioned against the H100
items.summary: ~780–800 TFLOPS FP16, 128 GB HBM (~3.2 TB/s aggregate), reaching ~80% of H100 dense FP16 throughput but trailing on efficiency (older 7nm, slower memory). The basis of CloudMatrix 384. Specs are from analyst reporting — treat as approximate.

items.title: CloudMatrix 384 (CM384)
items.innovation: 384× 910C supernode — answer to NVIDIA GB200 NVL72
items.summary: 384 910C NPUs across 16 racks deliver ~300 PFLOPS BF16 and ~49 TB HBM (≈2x compute, ~3.5x HBM of NVL72), fully meshed via 6,912 400G optical links — but at ~559 kW and ~2.3x power-per-FLOP. Brute-force scale compensating for weaker per-chip efficiency.

items.title: Atlas 800T A2 / A3 servers
items.innovation: The 8-NPU training server SKU frameworks target
items.summary: 'Atlas A2 Training series' (910B) is the baseline hardware string throughout vllm-ascend, verl, and MindSpeed docs; the newer Atlas A3 (910C) is the latest training/inference tier. This is the box most RL/training stacks validate against.

items.title: Huawei vs NVIDIA: scale vs efficiency
items.innovation: Wins on aggregate scale; trails on efficiency & ecosystem
items.summary: Per-chip the 910C ≈ 80% of an H100 on FP16; at rack scale CM384 can exceed NVL72 on raw compute/memory. Huawei closes the silicon gap via supernode aggregation while still trailing on process node, HBM generation, power, and especially software maturity.

items.title: CANN (Compute Architecture for Neural Networks)
items.innovation: Ascend's full compute SDK — analogue of CUDA+cuDNN+NCCL
items.summary: Layered driver/runtime/libraries including AOL acceleration libs, the HCCL collective-comms library (≈NCCL), the OPP operator package, and graph/offline engines. Huawei announced a full open-source release of the Ascend software stack in August 2025.

items.title: AscendCL (ACL) / pyACL
items.innovation: User-facing runtime API (≈ CUDA Runtime/Driver API)
items.summary: The C/C++ (and Python via pyACL) application layer of CANN handling device/context/stream/memory plus model & operator execution. Apps load and run models or single operators through it.

items.title: Ascend C (kernel language)
items.innovation: C++ kernel/custom-operator language (≈ writing CUDA kernels)
items.summary: A two-tier API over the AI Core's cube (matrix) and vector units, used to author fused/custom operators absent from the prebuilt OPP. Operator authoring here is where most CUDA→Ascend porting effort concentrates.

items.title: Relationship to CUDA
items.innovation: Parallel, non-CUDA-compatible stack — port, don't recompile
items.summary: There is no CUDA source/binary compatibility: HCCL substitutes for NCCL, Ascend C for CUDA kernels, AscendCL for the CUDA runtime. The lack of a CUDA shim is the single biggest source of porting friction.

items.title: MindSpore
items.innovation: In-house DL framework with first-class Ascend integration
items.summary: Offers the deepest Ascend optimization and hybrid-parallel support, but a smaller ecosystem than PyTorch. Underpins the MindSpore-Lab model suites (MindFormers, MindNLP).

items.title: torch_npu (PyTorch on Ascend)
items.innovation: Official out-of-tree PyTorch backend exposing NPUs as a device
items.summary: Lets existing PyTorch code run on Ascend via the 'npu' device and ops like torch_npu.npu_fusion_attention (the FlashAttention substitute). The foundation nearly all third-party LLM/RL frameworks (verl, MindSpeed, torchtune, LLaMA-Factory) build on.

items.title: MindSpeed-LLM (formerly ModelLink)
items.innovation: Megatron-style end-to-end LLM pretrain/finetune on Ascend
items.summary: Supports dataset prep, full-parameter and LoRA finetuning, and fused operators (attn, rmsnorm, swiglu, RoPE), with recent Qwen3-Next + Triton fusion adds. Megatron-LM ported via patches onto torch_npu.

items.title: MindSpeed (acceleration library)
items.innovation: Ascend-native fused kernels + parallelism (≈ Megatron-Core + apex)
items.summary: Provides Megatron-compatible distributed primitives (TP/PP/DP/EP, sequence parallel) optimized for Ascend, which MindSpeed-LLM and MindSpeed-RL sit on top of.

items.title: MindFormers (MindSpore Transformers)
items.innovation: Full-lifecycle transformer suite on MindSpore
items.summary: Ships ready configs for Llama2/Llama3-70B and DeepSeek-V3-671B with rich hybrid parallelism and one-click multi-node launch. The native-MindSpore alternative to the PyTorch/MindSpeed-LLM path.

items.title: Torchtune on Ascend
items.innovation: PyTorch-native finetuning with upstreamed Ascend backend
items.summary: Demonstrates LoRA/full finetuning on Ascend via torch_npu, validated in an official PyTorch blog post. Signals growing upstream (non-Huawei-repo) recognition of Ascend.

items.title: LLaMA-Factory on Ascend
items.innovation: Popular finetuning UI/CLI with Ascend NPU support via torch_npu
items.summary: Widely-used SFT/LoRA/DPO finetuning toolkit that gained Ascend NPU support, lowering the barrier to fine-tuning open models on 910B. A common on-ramp before moving to dedicated RL stacks.

items.title: vLLM-Ascend
items.innovation: Official community plugin — de-facto open inference path
items.summary: Supports dense, MoE, embedding, and multimodal LLMs with large-scale Expert Parallelism on Atlas A2/A3; requires CANN 9.x + torch-npu and tracks DeepSeek-V3/R1 closely. Also the rollout/generation engine most RL frameworks use on Ascend.

items.title: MindIE
items.innovation: First-party high-performance inference engine
items.summary: Huawei's production inference engine for Ascend; its MindIE-Turbo kernels integrate into vLLM-Ascend (pip install vllm-ascend[mindie-turbo]) for extra speed. The path Huawei Cloud / ModelArts production deployments use.

items.title: SGLang on Ascend
items.innovation: RadixAttention-style serving with emerging Ascend support
items.summary: Adds structured-output/RadixAttention serving on Ascend; less mature than vLLM-Ascend but used as the rollout engine for some RL stacks (e.g., slime, AReaL). GLM-5 was demonstrated on Ascend via SGLang.

items.title: MindSpeed-RL
items.innovation: Native distributed RL post-training for Ascend clusters
items.summary: Does GRPO-family RL on Qwen2.5-Dense-7B/32B, Qwen3-MoE-30B, and DeepSeek-R1-MoE-671B, with experiments on a 384-NPU superpod. The strongest evidence that large-scale R1-style RL is reproducible on Ascend, and the lowest-risk native path.
items.analysis: Innovation: Huawei's native distributed RL post-training stack for Ascend clusters, implementing GRPO-family algorithms with Ascend-native parallelism and kernels. Performance: demonstrated RL up to DeepSeek-R1-MoE-671B on a 384-NPU superpod — the strongest large-scale RL-on-Ascend evidence to date. Fit for RL-on-NPU: it's the first-party reference for what works at scale on Ascend; pair it with verl-on-Ascend as the two stacks any NPU RL benchmark should compare.

items.title: verl on Ascend NPU
items.innovation: Upstream open RLHF on Ascend via FSDP + vLLM-Ascend
items.summary: Validated SFT (Qwen2.5-0.5B) and GRPO (Qwen2-7B, Qwen2.5-VL-3B) on 8/16-NPU configs with a published DeepSeek-R1-Zero recipe. Key constraints: no FlashAttention (uses npu_fusion_attention) and no vLLM sleep mode yet, blocking the colocated hybrid engine.

items.title: AReaL (ascend branch)
items.innovation: Fully-async RL with a dedicated Ascend branch (stable ~Jan 2026)
items.summary: Decouples rollout from training (~2.77x speedup) and ships an 'ascend' branch marked stable. Async design is especially attractive on Ascend, where the missing sleep-mode/hybrid-engine limits colocated synchronous RL.

items.title: ROLL on Ascend
items.innovation: Multi-role Ray RL with a dedicated Ascend usage guide
items.summary: Provides first-party Ascend NPU documentation (alongside NVIDIA and AMD), covering RLHF/RLVR and multi-turn agentic RL. One of the strongest open-framework Ascend stories next to verl.

items.title: TRL on Ascend
items.innovation: HF GRPO/DPO trainers run on Ascend via torch_npu
items.summary: 910B GRPO confirmed working via vLLM-Ascend; TRL inherits the Transformers/Accelerate device layer for NPU support. The easiest on-ramp for small-scale RL fine-tuning on Ascend.

items.title: DeepSeek-R1-Zero reproduction on Ascend
items.innovation: GRPO R1-Zero recipes demonstrated at both small and large scale
items.summary: verl added a 'DeepSeek-R1-Zero on Ascend NPU' recipe (single/few nodes) and MindSpeed-RL reports RL of the full R1-MoE-671B on a 384-NPU superpod. Both small-scale and large-scale R1-style reproductions exist on Ascend.

items.title: AsyncFlow
items.innovation: Asynchronous streaming RL post-training framework
items.summary: Addresses the throughput limits of synchronous/colocated RL — directly relevant given Ascend's missing sleep-mode/hybrid-engine. Represents the research frontier of making RL efficient under NPU constraints.

items.title: Operator / kernel coverage gaps
items.innovation: Long-tail ops missing; manual Ascend C kernels required
items.summary: Auto-conversion covers only ~65% of PyTorch ops and the aclNN/Ascend C stack lacks long-tail operators, so porters hand-write kernels (host-side tiling + device kernel, no implicit caches). FlashAttention specifically must be replaced by npu_fusion_attention.

items.title: No upstream flash-attn / FA3 gated
items.innovation: Must swap in Huawei's npu_fusion_attention; FA3 not public
items.summary: The standard flash-attn package can't install on Ascend; community PRs wired npu_fusion_attention (FA2-equivalent) into Transformers, with recurring breakage. FlashAttention-3 for Ascend stayed gated as the required flash_attn_npu package wasn't publicly released.

items.title: Performance / MFU shortfall
items.innovation: Achieved throughput trails GPU and nominal peak
items.summary: In verl RL workloads, Ascend-vs-GPU throughput was ~0.59 for Qwen2.5-7B and 0.38–0.47 for Qwen2.5-VL; whole 'all-in-one' boxes cited at ~60–70% of H100. The 910B has ~60% of H100 compute but only ~36% of its memory bandwidth, making it more compute-bound.

items.title: Numerical precision / accuracy drift
items.innovation: Loss divergence from operator-semantic & accumulation differences
items.summary: Differences in op semantics, mixed-precision accumulation, optimizers, and RNG cause loss divergence and require manual precision alignment (CANN-specific loss scaling). verl's DAPO showed reward MAE ~3.7–4.3% on Ascend vs 0.3–3.3% for GRPO — algorithm-dependent drift.
items.analysis: What it is: loss/curve divergence on Ascend vs GPU from operator-semantic differences and mixed-precision accumulation order (fused attention, reductions, RNG). Why it matters: it can silently degrade RL convergence or final accuracy even when throughput looks fine — and it's hard to attribute. Implication: this is exactly what the align-probe tool targets — fix the rollout, compare training-forward logits/KL/advantages step-by-step, and attribute drift to a specific op; turning this into a reproducible diagnostic is a core contribution of the plan.

items.title: Missing vLLM sleep mode / hybrid engine
items.innovation: The single biggest RL-specific blocker on Ascend
items.summary: Without vLLM sleep mode, you can't memory-efficiently colocate the actor and rollout engine on the same NPUs, capping per-node model size and forcing disaggregated or smaller-model setups. Fixing this (or maturing async frameworks) is the highest-leverage improvement.
items.analysis: What it is: on CUDA vLLM, 'sleep mode' frees rollout-engine memory between phases so the trainer can reuse it (colocated hybrid engine); vLLM-Ascend lacks this. Why it matters: it's the single biggest RL-specific blocker on Ascend — without it you must offload or time-slice rollout vs train, costing throughput and memory headroom. Implication: it's why async/disaggregated RL (AReaL, AsyncFlow) and aggressive offloading are the pragmatic paths on 910B today; closing this gap is a concrete high-impact systems project.

items.title: Ecosystem maturity & tooling
items.innovation: Chinese-first docs, smaller community, parallel toolchain
items.summary: Core docs/forums center on the Chinese-language Ascend Community; you learn a parallel toolset (msprof, msSanitizer, msDebug) instead of Nsight/cuda-gdb, and hit HCCL collective-comm errors less battle-tested than NCCL. DeepSeek reportedly could not complete a stable R2 Ascend training run, switching back to NVIDIA for training while keeping Ascend for inference.
